Off Calendar Nov-15-2011 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Walter Rich appeared for Katherine Ewing (Social Worker) Family Service Agency. Richard Koch in Propria Persona.

Mr. Rich requested a continuance on the issue of change of Trustee stating that Barbara Simon was unable to take on Mr. Colombat and that Ms. Ewing will be leaving the agency next week. Status of the accounting was taken off calendar. Status of change of Trustee was continued to January 3,2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Oct-25-2011 Continued To Nov-15-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Koch (Trustee) in Propria Persona. Dr. Ralph Fenn Psychiatrist from Family Services Agency. Catherine Ewing Social Worker from Family Services Agency.

Dr. Fenn and Ms. Ewing expressed their concerns with regard to the relationship between the Trustee and Mr. Colombat, the Trustee not providing funds and the affect Mr. Koch's actions are having on Mr. Colombat. Mr. Koch is willing to yield to Barbara Simon coming in as Trustee. The matter was continued to November 15, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the 4th account/4th and Final and status of change of Trustee. The Court informed Mr. Koch that consent of Barbara Simon was needed. Mr. Koch is to provide a check in the amount of $175.00 payable to Catherine Ewing today and here after on the first of every month.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

No appearance was made by the Trustee who is in Propria Persona. A Notice to Appear will be issued to the Trustee Richard Koch to appear on September 20, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he should not be sanctioned $25.00 per day for failure to file the 4th Account. If the accounting is not filed September 20, 2011, sanctions will begin to run on that date.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-29-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Koch in Propria Persona.

Mr. Koch provided copies of bank statements and checks to the Court this morning. Mr. Koch stated that the $600.00 check payable to Henry Colombat was money requested by Mr. Colombat for his birhtday, that the $75.00 check listed for sundries was a mistake, that it was payable to himself for tax return preparation, and reported as to Mr. Colombat's current condition. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Rosada.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-31-2010 Continued To Sep-28-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Koch in Propria Persona.

Mr. Koch stated that he had no luck with obtaining statements from Family Court Yard and that he filed a further declaration. The Court stated that the receipts did not match the expenses, questioned how Mr. Colombat was able to cash a check payable to Family Court Yard and ordered Mr. Koch to file original bank statements and cancelled checks for this account period by September 21, 2010. The matter was continued to September 28, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-20-2010 Continued To Aug-31-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Koch in Propria Persona.

Mr. Koch stated that he attempted to get something from Family Court Yard, that they have not responded, that he got a statement from Henry Colombat and that he has no further receipts. Mr. Koch stated that he would prepare a declaration with regard to his efforts with Family Court Yard and that he would do his best to match receipts by August 26, 2010. The matter was continued to August 31, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a further declaration regarding $2,800.00 for sundries and clothing, cash paid to Family Court Yard, receipts and statements from Family Court Yard.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Y. Watts.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-22-2010 Continued To Jul-20-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Koch in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that the amendment does not address the receipts that did not match. The Court informed Mr. Koch once again that it wanted to see statements from Family Courtyard for the amounts of the cash allowances for the time Mr. Colombat was there, and matching receipts for clothing/sundries and expenditures. Mr. Koch stated that he could provide cancelled checks, that he would ask Family Courtyard to provide records and that he would go over the receipts for the sundries and clothing expenditures. The matter was continued to July 20, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of said records/receipts.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-20-2010 Continued To Jun-22-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Koch in Propria Persona as Trustee.

Mr. Koch stated that he had a waiver of notice from Henry Colombat, that he had to do a lot of work on the accounting and that he was amending schedule F in the accounting. Mr. Koch also addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes with regard to the receipts submitted/lack of receipts. The Court informed Mr. Koch that it wanted to see further statements from Family Court Yard for the amounts of the cash allowances for the time Mr. Colombat was there, matching receipts for clothing/sundries and statements of current balances. The matter was continued to June 22, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR#5520. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Bloom.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-28-2007 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Procedural Note: The matter was called at 9:20 a.m. but no appearance was made and the matter was passed. The matter was then called and heard at 10:20 a.m.

Appearances: Richard Koch (Trustee in pro per).

At the outset Mr. Koch noted his concern that the Register of Action ostensibly showed Kevin Urbatsch as his attorney even though he substituted out of the action in September 2005. The matter is to be reviewed.

Moving on to the accounting, the Court noted that due to the number of times the accounting was amended with certain procedural matters as yet unresolved to the Examiner's satisfaction, it would allow the accounting but would not approve it. The Court also provided some guidance on the preparation of future accountings with particular note to the unfavorable view of cash disbursements, out-of-pocket expenditures, and noticing amendments. The court also referred Mr. Koch to accountants who are knowledgeable in Probate accountings. The Court granted the Petition allowing but not approving the accounting and set the next annual accounting status date for August 8, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. Mr Koch is to prepare the Order.

Judicial Office: Dorothy L. McMath, Commissioner; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: Deputy Rigo Guitron; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: None.

MINI MINUTES: Richard Koch appearing as trustee in pro per. Court informs Mr. Koch that the notice was defective. He will need to file an amended petition and give 30 day notice. His accounting is not in proper format. Mr. Koch has spoken to Mr. Siracusa regarding the accounting format and he will speak to the examiner regarding that issue. Matter is continued to 8/9.
